title: Goose
oneLiner: Open-source desktop, CLI, and API agent from Block for coding and everyday workflows.
summary: Goose is Block's open-source AI agent for working across code, local workflows, tools, and extensions through desktop, CLI, and API surfaces.
whyItMatters: Goose matters because it treats agent work as something that belongs on the user's machine, connected to local files, development tools, and extensions rather than trapped inside a chat tab.
bestFor: Developers who want a local agent for coding and workflow tasks
Teams evaluating desktop and CLI agent UX
Builders interested in MCP-style extension ecosystems
notFor: Users who want a fully managed consumer product with no setup work
Teams that cannot review the linked source, license, and operational requirements before adoption
seoTitle: Goose: Agents resource for open AI builders
seoDescription: Goose profile on OpenAgent.bot: what it is, when to use it, official links, open-source status, and structured resource data.
shareDescription: Open-source desktop, CLI, and API agent from Block for coding and everyday workflows.

coreStrengths.title: Local agent surface
coreStrengths.description: Goose provides desktop and CLI paths for agent work.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Local surfaces are often more natural for coding, files, and repeatable workflows.

coreStrengths.title: Extension-oriented design
coreStrengths.description: Goose connects agents to tools through extensions and provider configuration.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Agents become more useful when they can safely reach the tools a user already relies on.

coreStrengths.title: Permissive open-source release
coreStrengths.description: Block has described Goose as Apache-2.0 open source.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: A permissive license makes it easier to study and adapt the agent runtime.

useCaseNotes.title: Developer task execution
useCaseNotes.description: Use Goose to inspect repos, run tools, and work through coding tasks locally.

useCaseNotes.title: MCP-backed workflows
useCaseNotes.description: Connect Goose to extensions or servers that expose tools to the agent.

useCaseNotes.title: Desktop agent evaluation
useCaseNotes.description: Compare what a desktop-first agent can do compared with a browser-only chat UI.

compareNotes.title: Goose fits local developer workflows
compareNotes.summary: Hosted agents can be convenient, but Goose is more interesting when local files, tools, and extension control matter.

commandLine.label: Clone the Goose repository
commandLine.description: Start from the official Block repository and follow the current install instructions in the docs.

seoArticle.intro: Goose is Block's open-source AI agent for working across code, local workflows, tools, and extensions through desktop, CLI, and API surfaces.
seoArticle.whatItIs: Goose is an open AI agents resource tracked by OpenAgent.bot because it gives builders a concrete implementation path rather than just a product claim.
seoArticle.whyItMatters: Goose matters because it treats agent work as something that belongs on the user's machine, connected to local files, development tools, and extensions rather than trapped inside a chat tab.
seoArticle.howItWorks: Start from the official repository or documentation, verify the license and runtime requirements, then test it on a narrow workflow before expanding it into production use.

faq.question: Is Goose open source?
faq.answer: Goose is listed with Apache-2.0 based on its official source links. Always re-check the repository or model card before production use.

faq.question: Who should evaluate Goose?
faq.answer: Developers who want a local agent for coding and workflow tasks
